Common Questions
Everything you need to know about leverage trading and liquidation.
What is a liquidation price in crypto leverage trading? ▼
The liquidation price is the price at which an exchange automatically closes your leveraged position to prevent losses exceeding your margin. With a long position using ×10 leverage, a price drop of just 10% from your entry triggers liquidation and you lose your entire margin deposit. CryptoLeverage calculates this instantly for any coin and leverage combination.
How is the liquidation price calculated? ▼
The formula is straightforward: Liquidation Price (Long) = Entry Price × (1 − 1/Leverage). For short positions: Entry Price × (1 + 1/Leverage). For example, entering Bitcoin at $66,000 with ×10 leverage long gives a liquidation price of $66,000 × (1 − 0.1) = $59,400. Note that real exchanges may apply additional maintenance margin fees.
What leverage is safe for crypto trading? ▼
Most professional traders recommend staying below ×5 leverage, especially for volatile assets like PEPE or DOGE. With ×5, you get liquidated at a 20% adverse move — which happens frequently in crypto. ×2 or ×3 is considered conservative. ×20 and above is extremely risky and typically used only for very short-term scalping with tight stop-losses.
Does CryptoLeverage support all cryptocurrencies? ▼
Yes — CryptoLeverage loads the top 1000 cryptocurrencies by market cap in real time using the CoinGecko API, including Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), PEPE, Shiba Inu, and hundreds more. Prices refresh automatically every 60 seconds. Use the search bar to find any coin instantly.
Is CryptoLeverage free to use? ▼
Completely free, forever. We're supported by optional affiliate links to trading exchanges — if you find this tool useful and open an account through our links, we earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. This keeps CryptoLeverage free and maintained.
What is the difference between isolated and cross margin? ▼
Isolated margin limits your loss to the margin you put into that specific position — you can't lose more than your stake. Cross margin uses your entire account balance as margin, which can prevent liquidation longer but risks your whole account. CryptoLeverage calculates based on isolated margin, which is the most common mode for beginners.
CryptoLeverage
⚠️ For educational purposes only. Liquidation prices vary by exchange due to maintenance margin, fees, and funding rates. Always verify on your exchange before trading. Not financial advice. Trading leveraged products involves significant risk of loss.
